current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: The hybrid_topk Adam stabilization produced a meaningful full improvement to 0.1619 FVU, but that is still far from the aggressive target. This round keeps the best family and optimizer fixed and spends one proxy run on a larger expansion factor as the cleanest quality-first scaling test.

The hybrid_topk Adam stabilization produced a meaningful full improvement to 0.1619 FVU, but that is still far from the aggressive target. This round keeps the best family and optimizer fixed and spends one proxy run on a larger expansion factor as the cleanest quality-first scaling test. full

The expansion_factor=16 follow-up did not improve full FVU and cost substantial throughput, so the next move should be a targeted architecture refinement rather than more brute-force scaling. This round keeps the winning hybrid_topk Adam ef=8 setup and upgrades the nonlinear branch to modulate linear logits as well as add residual logits.

action.hypothesis: hybrid_topk has now beaten the K=128 full baseline, so the next quality-first move is to stabilize the same family with an optimizer better suited to its smooth nonlinear residual branch. Test hybrid_topk at K=128 with Adam to see whether denser adaptive updates improve FVU beyond the current full frontier.
action.summary: hybrid_topk is the first family to improve the K=128 full frontier, but the gain is still small relative to the aggressive quality target. This round keeps the architecture fixed and changes only the optimizer to probe whether the family is currently undertrained rather than intrinsically capped.
action.self_review: This is a disciplined follow-up because the family finally showed real full-run promise and deserves one quality-focused stabilization pass before opening more architectures. Adam may not help, but it tests a concrete training-dynamics hypothesis without confounding the model design.
notes_to_memory: hybrid_topk is now the best full K=128 frontier at FVU 0.18129383772611618, narrowly beating the previous topk anchor 0.18532327562570572.
Round 8 keeps focus on hybrid_topk and tests whether Adam can better optimize its nonlinear residual branch than signum.
next_hypotheses: If hybrid_topk plus Adam improves proxy FVU, promote it to full as the new mainline family candidate.
If hybrid_topk plus Adam is flat or worse, keep the family but try a higher-capacity quality pass such as larger expansion_factor before considering cost reduction or lower K.

action.hypothesis: hybrid_topk with Adam is now the strongest K=128 configuration, so the next quality-first move is to increase representational capacity rather than shrink K or chase cost. Test hybrid_topk at K=128 with Adam and expansion_factor=16 to see whether extra latent capacity can materially reduce FVU again.
action.summary: The hybrid_topk Adam stabilization produced a meaningful full improvement to 0.1619 FVU, but that is still far from the aggressive target. This round keeps the best family and optimizer fixed and spends one proxy run on a larger expansion factor as the cleanest quality-first scaling test.
action.self_review: This is the right next bet because the current mainline finally has momentum and deserves capacity scaling before any lower-K or cost tradeoffs. The main risk is that extra capacity may give only marginal quality gains relative to added memory and compute.
notes_to_memory: hybrid_topk plus Adam improved the full K=128 frontier to FVU 0.16189537942409515 with no runtime regression relative to the prior hybrid run.
Round 9 escalates capacity within the leading family by testing expansion_factor=16 at fixed K=128 and Adam before considering any lower-K or cost-driven moves.
next_hypotheses: If hybrid_topk with Adam and expansion_factor=16 improves proxy FVU, promote it to full as the new mainline quality candidate.
If the larger expansion factor is flat or only marginally better, keep hybrid_topk as mainline and look for a more targeted architectural improvement rather than scaling capacity further.

action.hypothesis: hybrid_topk may now be limited by using the nonlinear branch only as an additive correction; letting it multiplicatively modulate the linear logits should improve support shaping while preserving the strong baseline path. Re-test hybrid_topk at the best-known K=128, expansion_factor=8, optimizer=adam setting after this targeted hybrid-family stabilization edit.
action.summary: The expansion_factor=16 follow-up did not improve full FVU and cost substantial throughput, so the next move should be a targeted architecture refinement rather than more brute-force scaling. This round keeps the winning hybrid_topk Adam ef=8 setup and upgrades the nonlinear branch to modulate linear logits as well as add residual logits.
action.self_review: This is a narrow, defensible follow-up on the current best family and it directly addresses the likely limit of the present hybrid design without reopening the broader search space. It still adds encoder complexity, so any quality gain will need later cost scrutiny against the ef=8 Adam mainline.
notes_to_memory: hybrid_topk with Adam and expansion_factor=16 achieved better proxy FVU but slightly worse full FVU (0.1623645443469286 vs 0.16189537942409515) and a clear throughput regression, so brute-force scaling is not the next priority.

Round 10 stabilizes hybrid_topk by adding a zero-initialized multiplicative modulation path on top of the existing additive nonlinear residual, keeping the model anchored to the proven linear encoder at initialization.
next_hypotheses: If the modulated hybrid_topk improves proxy FVU, promote it to full as the next mainline quality candidate.

If the modulated hybrid_topk is flat or worse, keep the current hybrid_topk Adam ef=8 checkpoint as mainline and revisit training dynamics or a genuinely new family instead of more local hybrid edits.

mixture_topk.design_hypothesis: A soft mixture-of-experts encoder may beat the current K=128 topk anchor by giving the encoder more routing expressivity without the hard-capacity bottlenecks of grouped routing. Test the existing mixture_topk family at the same K=128 and expansion factor as a quality-first proxy comparison.
next_steps: If mixture_topk shows even modest proxy FVU gain, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before judging promotion.
If mixture_topk is flat or worse under normal runtime, try another expressive family such as residual_topk or factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor.

residual_topk.design_hypothesis: A residual two-branch encoder may reduce FVU at K=128 by letting the second encoder pass target what the first branch misses, which is directly aligned with the reconstruction objective. Test residual_topk at the same K=128 anchor point as a quality-first architecture probe.
next_steps: If residual_topk shows proxy improvement, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before promotion.
If residual_topk is also flat or worse, try factorized_topk next rather than returning to smaller-K search.

factorized_topk.design_hypothesis: The stabilized factorized_topk crash was caused by an implementation bug in the widened hidden-path projection, not by the architecture itself. Fix the hidden-to-latent projection and rerun the same K=128 factorized_topk stabilization pass to recover a valid quality signal.
next_steps: If the fixed factorized_topk now yields a valid proxy improvement, keep incubating the family with quality-focused tuning.
If the fixed factorized_topk still performs poorly under normal runtime, stop spending rounds on current built-in families and move to a genuinely new encoder design.

next_move_guidance: If the modulated hybrid_topk improves proxy FVU, promote it to full as the next mainline quality candidate.

If the modulated hybrid_topk is flat or worse, keep the current hybrid_topk Adam ef=8 checkpoint as mainline and revisit training dynamics or a genuinely new family instead of more local hybrid edits.
